title: APK Archaeology findings.json
description: Formal contract for findings.json, the headline source-of-truth artifact emitted by the apk-archaeology skill for a single run. Encodes the exact shape of the canonical instance at run-2026-07-17. Each property carries a custom `x-source` annotation (deterministic | synthesized | mixed) recording whether emit_findings.py derives it from data/*.json, an agent authors it, or it's a blend of both -- this is the machine-readable form of design §3 (honest sync reach): what the deterministic sync (emit/render + selftest) owns vs. what stays agent-authored. `x-source` is descriptive metadata only; it never affects validation.

migrationBucket.description: One migration-shape bucket: what falls into it, its rough size, and a qualifying note.

metricGroup.description: Base shape shared by metrics sub-groups: integer counts plus a qualifying note. Individual groups override required/properties to their own fields.

schema_version.description: Version tag of this findings.json contract, e.g. "0.1-pilot". Fixed by tooling, not authored per-run.

run.description: Run identity and provenance. package/version/date/id are deterministic (read off the APK/run folder); sha256 and tooling are deterministic (computed/recorded by the toolchain); mode is contextual -- set by the operator/agent describing what pass(es) actually ran.

version.description: APK version name (and optionally version code).

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the analyzed APK. CLI-provided (passed as a --sha256 override to emit_findings.py), not derived from data/*.json.

tooling.description: Toolchain versions used for this run (e.g. jadx, apktool, python). CLI-provided (passed as a --tooling override to emit_findings.py), not derived from data/*.json.

mode.description: Which passes ran (static-only, static+dynamic, etc.) and why, if constrained.

verdict.description: The headline call: is the migration feasible, in one line, and how confident is the analyst.

what_it_is.description: What the app actually is under the hood. nature and build_signal are narrative framing, synthesized by an agent; first_party_mass and obfuscated_island are also agent-filled -- classify.json carries no per-package file counts, so none of this group is emitter-derivable today.

nature.description: Narrative characterization of the codebase (bespoke vs. white-label platform, etc.).

first_party_mass.description: Open map of first-party package-path prefix -> file count. Not emitter-derivable -- classify.json carries no per-package file counts; agent-filled until classify emits counts. May carry an optional narrative note.

note.description: Optional qualifying note about the package-count breakdown.

obfuscated_island.description: The (if any) obfuscated/third-party package island found outside the first-party mass. package and files are agent-filled, not emitter-derivable.

package.description: Not emitter-derivable -- classify.json carries no per-package file counts; agent-filled until classify emits counts.

files.description: Not emitter-derivable -- classify.json carries no per-package file counts; agent-filled until classify emits counts.

build_signal.description: Narrative read of build/debug signals (LeakCanary, debug activities, minSdk/target, etc.).

migration_shape.description: The four-bucket migration classification an agent synthesizes from partitions/flows: what ports natively, what stays WebView, what bridge surface must be reimplemented, and what is generic platform/infra tax.

metrics.description: Counts derived by emit_findings.py where a data/*.json carries them; bridge_capabilities and bridge_pilot_handlers are agent-filled (see their x-source).

blind_spots.description: Known gaps/unknowns in this run's coverage, authored by the analyzing agent.

next_steps.description: Recommended follow-up actions, authored by the analyzing agent.

manifest.description: Open map of artifact relative-path -> {role, status}. Paths and status are structural/deterministic (produced by the run's file layout and its verification state); role is an authored one-line description of what the artifact is for.

role.description: One-line authored description of the artifact's purpose.

status.description: Verification state of the artifact (e.g. verified, raw, current).

caveats.description: Cross-cutting qualifications an analyst attaches when citing this run's numbers elsewhere.
